brief_summary: Seven-row by three-column matrix comparing what's included at each of the three purchasing tiers — prebuilt, barebones kit, loose-parts DIY — across case, plate, PCB, mounting hardware, stabilizers, switches, and keycaps. Filled dots mark included items, an amber-tinted half-fill marks vendor-dependent inclusion, open dots mark items the buyer must source separately.
data_sources: custom-keyboard-kit-buyers-guide.mdx § 'Prebuilt, barebones, or loose parts' — 'A prebuilt board ships complete — case, plate, PCB, switches, keycaps, stabilizers, cable, ready to plug in and type on. A barebones kit ships the structural core — case, plate, PCB, mounting hardware — and stops there. The buyer supplies switches and keycaps separately, and often the stabilizers too.'

custom-keyboard-kit-buyers-guide.mdx § 'Prebuilt, barebones, or loose parts' — 'Loose-parts DIY means sourcing the case, the plate, and the PCB from separate vendors individually, with no guarantee any two of them were designed against the same tolerances.'
